No web results. I'll rely on local evidence and the kinetic principle I know: the integrated amyloid mass fraction follows roughly `dc/dt ∝ (plateau - c) · (κ·c + λ)` where the secondary nucleation contribution scales as `c·(c+offset)` giving a sigmoidal shape. The best sibling's cubic-in-c polynomial works well.

My strategy: **exploit the best sibling family (autonomous cubic, 0.8910)** but add one targeted improvement. The best sibling Program 1/3 already used onset = (c1²+c2²·seed)·c8²·time — a time-driven onset. But Program 1 (onset with time) and Program 3 are identical at 0.8910. Program 2 (pure autonomous, no time) scored 0.8701.

So the **time-driven onset gated by capacity** helps. The current parent (0.8785) is structurally similar but has linear-only growth (no c² secondary, no c³ sharpening). The proven win is: **add the c² secondary nucleation and c³ sharpening terms** to the parent while keeping its time onset.
